prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-visibility image. The setting and objects are difficult to distinguish; only vague dark shapes are visible, with no clearly identifiable people or products.

By the middle of the clip, the scene changes to a wintry, blue-toned outdoor setting. A house with lit windows appears in the background, surrounded by snow. In the foreground or central area, a person is visible with arms extended outward, wearing dark/red clothing. The image has a soft, layered or dreamlike quality, as if multiple visuals are superimposed. Near the lower part of the frame, small dark animal shapes are visible; they appear to be dogs or similar animals, but the details are not fully clear.

At the end, the commercial cuts to a close-up tabletop arrangement. A Hallmark-branded item, likely a card or folder, lies prominently in the center. It has a red band at the top, a gold crown emblem, and the word “Hallmark” in red script. Surrounding it are decorative or personal items, including what looks like a gold chain or ornament, papers, and other household objects. Large white on-screen text reads: “There’s no place like Hallmark.”
